user_message: Brian Yamamoto at Alpine Ski House just sent this. Help me generate the watchlist.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-23 10:39 EST
Subject: Follow-up: security posture Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with security posture. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
Date: 2024-02-04 17:06 EST
Subject: Re: security posture Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for security posture. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-18 08:30 EST
Subject: Re: security posture Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the security posture proposal. Our COO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Alpine Ski House: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF39-E trap (MSX proposals duplicate already-tracked items); produces complete Consolidated decision log, cross-thread commitment inventory, action list with thread attribution, M
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF39-E failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF39-E failure mode (MSX proposals duplicate already-tracked items)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF39-E -- MSX proposals duplicate already-tracked items
